summary: Second architecture review accepts the REQUEST_CHANGES fixes. BootSimulationInputV1 now rejects mixed fixture and runtime-content-pack mode fields before branch selection; the concrete ambiguous payload rejects with invalid-payload at path $ and returns no runtime/hash. Regression coverage exists in the sim-core boot-boundary contract test. The prior scope finding is resolved by explicit task allowed_paths authorization for lead-owned orchestration recovery files with a limiting note.

tests_run.result: Worktree contains protocol/sim-core/test changes, lead-authorized orchestration state files, prior and revised handoff/route messages, and this pending second architect review handoff.

Concrete mixed payload { protocolVersion: 1, fixture: minimal-m1, source: runtime-content-pack, seed: 1531, runtimeContentPack: { invalid: true } } returned status rejected with error code invalid-payload at path $, and did not return runtime or hash.

7 test files passed; 35 tests passed. Regression coverage in tests/sim-core-boot-boundary.test.ts confirms mixed fixture plus runtime-content-pack payload rejects. Existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning appeared for deterministic-rng-worker.ts.

tests_run.command: node allowed-path check from task scope
tests_run.result: 17 changed/untracked files checked against task allowed_paths and forbidden_paths; disallowed=[], forbiddenHits=[].
1 protocol schema test file passed; 3 tests passed.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 15 expected violation(s).
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.

decisions: ACCEPT: ARCH-BOOT-001 is resolved by mutually exclusive boot-mode validation and bootSimulationV1-level regression coverage.

ACCEPT: ARCH-SCOPE-001 is resolved for this review package because the task allowed_paths now explicitly include project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, and project/tasks/thread-registry.json, with a note limiting them to orchestration recovery evidence only.

The sim-core/content-runtime boundary remains acceptable: sim-core receives a plain runtimeContentPack DTO, validates it internally, and does not import content-runtime/content-source or host/presentation APIs.
No economy, population, route-choice, UI, desktop, or broader product scope expansion was observed in the reviewed changes.
risks: The public boot DTO still relies on convention rather than a named mode field, but the parser now enforces mutually exclusive fixture versus runtime-content-pack fields before branch selection.
Lead-owned orchestration state files are in this task scope only for recovery evidence; they should not be treated as precedent for implementation-scope expansion in future tasks.
The M2 boot adapter still maps route baseTravelCost into existing RouteDefinition.lengthInMapUnits as a boundary placeholder; future transport/route-choice behavior needs a separate spec and task.
requested_action: Proceed with lead integration routing for M2-BOOT-BOUNDARY-001. Architecture review accepts the second package; keep QA/release checks separate per normal task flow.
